dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9387 IH: Recognizing Engineered Alternatives as Lab-Created Butter Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Wied (for himself, Mr. Riley of New York, Mr. Van Orden, Ms. Tenney, Mr. Rose, Mr. Schweikert, and Mr. Tiffany) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To amend the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to ensure consumer choice by requiring truthful labeling of lab-created butter, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Recognizing Engineered Alternatives as Lab-Created Butter Act or the REAL Butter Act.

section.header: Ensuring consumer choice by requiring truthful labeling of lab-created butter
section.text: Section 403 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 343) is amended by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: If it is a synthesized butter product or contains such a product and its label does not bear immediately before the name of the product lab-created butter or contains lab-created butter, respectively.

subparagraph.text: The term ‘synthesized butter product’ means a product that—

clause.text: is marketed as butter, but utilizes sources of milkfat that are synthesized through non-agricultural processes; and
fails to conform to the standard of identity for butter specified in the Act of March 4, 1923 (42 Stat. 1500).

subparagraph.text: The term butter has the meaning given such term in the Act of March 4, 1923 (42 Stat. 1500).
